About
Talk to an engineer

The reliability blind spot

The assets you can't monitor
are the ones most likely to fail.Electrical Signature Analysis changes that.

30–40% of critical rotating equipment operates without condition monitoring. Not because it's been overlooked. Because sensors can't reach the assets: submerged pumps, sealed blowers, hazardous zones, remote infrastructure.

Monitoring 10,000+ industrial assets across 80+ operators

Speak with an engineer Evaluate your assets

The gap

The monitoring gap has real operational costs

Assets below the monitoring line aren't low priority. They are critical rotating machines operating in environments where conventional sensors cannot be deployed. The result is a monitoring gap with measurable operational consequences.

30–40% of critical rotating equipment operates without condition monitoring Industry estimate
£390K fine avoided through early fault detection Yorkshire Water
€50K+ cost avoided per unplanned shutdown ArcelorMittal
15,000 manual inspection trips eliminated per year Yorkshire Water

These costs exist because many critical machines operate where conventional monitoring systems cannot be deployed.

The cost of inaction

Failures cost far more than the repair

Failures in unmonitored assets trigger costs across operations, compliance, safety, and energy use.

Unplanned downtime

A single remote pump failure often exceeds €50,000 in emergency repair, lost production, and overtime. Across a fleet, the exposure quickly reaches millions per year.

Environmental fines

A sewage pump failure can trigger a spill. Fines alone can reach £390,000, excluding remediation costs and regulatory investigations.

Safety exposure

Manual inspections in confined spaces, ATEX zones, or submerged environments expose personnel to risk. The safest inspection is the one that doesn't require site access.

Hidden energy loss

A pump running 5–10% below optimal efficiency wastes thousands in electricity each year. Across hundreds of motors, the loss becomes substantial.

Why the gap exists

Why many assets remain unmonitored

Conventional condition monitoring requires sensors installed on the asset. In many environments, installing those sensors is impractical. Three barriers prevent it.

01

Physical access

Submersible pumps operate 20+ metres underwater. Sealed blowers sit inside enclosed housings. High-temperature motors destroy sensors within months. You cannot install sensors where you cannot reach the machine.

Submerged pumps Enclosed drives High-temperature motors
02

Economics

A vibration sensor costs $500. Installed on a submerged pump, the cost exceeds $5,000 once scaffolding, permits, shutdowns, and specialist labour are included. At that price, monitoring rarely scales across the fleet.

$500 → $5,000+ installed Scaffolding and permits Production shutdowns
03

Regulation

ATEX zones require explosion-proof equipment and certified installation. Confined space entry demands permits, standby teams, and strict procedures. Routine sensor deployment becomes impractical.

ATEX certification Confined space permits Hot work procedures

Together, these barriers leave a large share of industrial assets without condition monitoring.

Read the proximity penalty analysis →

The shift

Monitor the drivetrain from the electrical cabinet

Electrical Signature Analysis reads the motor's current and voltage at the MCC. No sensor touches the asset.

AI models detect faults across the full drivetrain: motor, coupling, gearbox, and driven load. Warnings arrive weeks to months before failure.

Every drivetrain component affects the motor's electrical signature. That is why a single measurement point at the cabinet can monitor the entire mechanical chain.

How ESA monitors the drivetrain →
Sensor on the asset
  • Requires physical access to the asset
  • Production shutdown needed to install
  • Blocked by heat, submersion, or confined space
  • One sensor monitors one component
ESA from the MCC
  • Installs at the MCC in under 30 minutes
  • No production shutdown, no asset access
  • Operates in any environment the motor can run in
  • One connection monitors the full drivetrain

“We invested heavily in condition-based maintenance — vibration, oil analysis, airflow, the lot. But those approaches can still be snapshots. SAM4 added continuous visibility, and that changes how confident you feel about what's happening.”

John McCrystal Reliability & Maintenance Specialist, DuPont

Find the assets your monitoring can't see

An asset audit identifies the machines your monitoring cannot reach. It maps your unmonitored fleet, prioritises the highest-risk assets, and provides the data needed to build a business case.

No installation or site visit required.

Start your asset audit Talk to an engineer
